Lead Source: Referral (Kyoto Spring Festival contact)

Show Type: High count
Priority: High
ADHOC Sales Contact: Marcus Chen
Show Description: Opening of Sakura Tech Expo 2025 at Banpaku Park, themed around the 50-year arc from Expo 1970 to the AI age. Drone show traces Japanese tech innovation history visually. 13 minutes.

Event Details
On Site Date(s): 2025-11-08 to 2025-11-14
Testing and Performance Date(s): Testing 2025-11-10, 2025-11-11. Performance 2025-11-12 19:00 local
ADHOC On-Site Producer: Carlos Mendez
Pilot and CoPilot: Captain Tom Henderson (PIC) and Captain Maya Foster (CoPilot), Skyburst Drones LLC holds JCAB waiver
Support Hands: 10 hands, ADHOC POC: Lila Nakamura, arrival 2025-11-07, departure 2025-11-15
Company Providing Drones/Batteries/Support Gear: Skyburst Drones LLC and Nightfall Aero (split deployment due to scale)

Permit Requirements and Timeline
Aviation Liability Insurance: 15M USD via Tokio Marine, policy TM-2025-JPN-0098
FAA: N/A - International venue. JCAB waiver JCAB-2025-OSK-DR-088 secured 2025-09-15
Pyrodrone Fire Marshal Permit: Not applicable
Local Fire Department Notice: Osaka Municipal Fire Bureau notified 2025-10-01
Local Police Notice: Osaka Prefectural Police notified 2025-10-05, traffic plan reviewed
Venue Requirements: Banpaku Park management approval received 2025-08-20, exclusivity confirmed
City Requirements: Osaka Prefecture event permit OSK-2025-EV-1244 approved 2025-09-20
Gear List
Drones: 700x mixed fleet (450x Skyburst Pro X9, 250x Nightfall Phoenix)
Batteries: 2,100x mixed pack inventory
Battery Chargers: 36x mixed MultiPort and HexCharge units
Power Source for Battery Chargers: 3x 60kW generators plus venue grid
Base Station: 2x command stations (one per fleet vendor) with cross-link sync
Media Capture Plan
ADHOC Responsible Party: Raj Kumar
Content Handoff Plan: 4K master plus 8K archival capture, NHK broadcast feed, social cuts within 24 hours, VR capture for press kit
Drone Show Debrief
Show executed on 2025-11-12 with park audience of 35,000 plus NHK broadcast reaching 4.5M viewers domestic. Largest ADHOC show by drone count and most complex multi-vendor coordination to date. Mixed-fleet sync between Skyburst and Nightfall systems required custom integration work that pushed schedule but performed cleanly on show night. Three drones lost (within tolerance). Financial outcome: 3% margin below target due to integration overhead. Lessons learned: multi-vendor shows require dedicated integration lead role we did not staff; future shows over 500 drones with multiple fleets should add 30 days lead time and a 0.5 FTE integration engineer for 6 weeks pre-show.
